name: Palo Alto XSOAR SOAR Platform API
what_it_does: Enterprise Security Orchestration, Automation and Response (SOAR) platform REST API from Palo Alto Networks — enabling agents to trigger security playbooks, manage incidents, query IOC data, and integrate with 800+ security tool integrations.
use_cases: Triggering security playbooks programmatically in response to external alerts
Creating and updating security incidents from external detection sources
Querying incident timelines and investigation findings for reporting agents
Automating IOC enrichment workflows across threat intelligence sources
Integrating SOAR playbook execution results into external SIEM or ticketing systems
not_for: Simple alert routing without complex playbook automation (use PagerDuty)
Organizations without security operations teams (SOAR requires SOC investment)
Small teams without dedicated security engineering for playbook development
best_when: A security operations center needs to automate incident response playbooks at scale with 800+ integration library and advanced orchestration capabilities.
avoid_when: You need lightweight alert management — XSOAR is enterprise SOAR that requires significant investment to deploy and operate.

auth.notes: API key passed as X-AUTH-TOKEN header. Keys generated per user or service account. Role-based access control on XSOAR operations. On-premise and cloud (XSIAM) deployment have different auth mechanisms.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition available for testing; limited to 3 users
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing from ~$50k-$500k+/year based on scale
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing by Palo Alto Networks. Community edition available for evaluation. XSIAM (cloud SIEM+SOAR) different pricing model. Significant investment required for enterprise deployment.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; on-premise depends on server capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation on xsoar.pan.dev. Python client library (demisto-client) available. REST API covers incidents, playbooks, automation scripts, and integrations. Error responses include reason codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident creation is not idempotent — duplicate incidents can be created. Query operations are idempotent. Playbook triggers are not — implement deduplication on external side.
known_agent_gotchas: On-premise vs cloud XSIAM have different API endpoints and behavior
Playbook execution is async — poll incident workplan for completion status
Integration commands run in separate containers — latency varies by integration
Community edition lacks many API features available in enterprise

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized (cloud). SOC2, ISO 27001. API key with header (not query param). RBAC on XSOAR operations. On-premise option for highest security control. Palo Alto Networks security standards apply.
